heading: Limitation on changes to the Social Security Act
label: 939 Limitation on changes to the Social Security Act
notes: References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (a), is , . children.heading: Limitation on changes to the Social Security Act
children.label: a Limitation on changes to the Social Security Act
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, it shall not be in order in the Senate or the House of Representatives to consider any bill or resolution pursuant to any expedited procedure to consider the recommendations of a Task Force for Responsible Fiscal Action or other commission that contains recommendations with respect to the old-age, survivors, and disability insurance program established under title II of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], or the taxes received under subchapter A of chapter 9; the taxes imposed by subchapter E of chapter 1; and the taxes collected under section 86 of part II of subchapter B of chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code.42 U.S.C. 401

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: b Waiver
children.text: This section may be waived or suspended in the Senate only by the affirmative vote of three-fifths of the Members, duly chosen and sworn.

children.heading: Appeals
children.label: c Appeals
children.text: An affirmative vote of three-fifths of the Members of the Senate, duly chosen and sworn, shall be required in the Senate to sustain an appeal of the ruling of the Chair on a point of order raised under this section.
